subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Functions Table — Chain Rule Tangent, Quotient Rule h'(1) & FTC with f''(2x)
stimulus:
The functions $f$ and $g$ have continuous second derivatives. The table gives values of the functions and their derivatives at selected values of $x$.

| $x$ | $f(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $g'(x)$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $-6$ | 3 | 2 | 8 |
| 2 | 2 | $-2$ | $-3$ | 0 |
| 3 | 8 | 7 | 6 | 2 |
| 6 | 4 | 5 | 3 | $-1$ |

parts.prompt: Let $k(x) = f(g(x))$. Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $k$ at $x = 3$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Tangent line equation: $y = 4 + 10(x - 3)$ or equivalent
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $k(3) = f(g(3)) = f(6) = 4$. $k'(3) = f'(6) \cdot g'(3) = 5 \cdot 2 = 10$. Tangent line: $y = 4 + 10(x-3)$.

rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Applies quotient rule: $h'(x) = \frac{f(x) \cdot g'(x) - g(x) \cdot f'(x)}{[f(x)]^2}$
1 pt: Uses $u$-substitution or chain rule: antiderivative of $f''(2x)$ is $\frac{1}{2}f'(2x)$
